quiz.question:
Black moves their king up to c7. 

Can the White king ever invade one of the key squares?

answers.text: No, because Black can defend against all attempts.

feedback.text: Exactly. Black would need to prevent White from getting the opposition or invading favorably.

distractors.text: Yes. Kd5 wins because the white king will enter one of the key squares.

feedback.text: If Kd5, Black gets the opposition and White will never invade. Remember that pushing the pawn won't help White.

distractors.text: Yes, Ke5 wins because it gets the diagonal opposition which will give White access to one of the key squares.

feedback.text: The diagonal opposition is meaningless here because the pawn on c4 can be attacked.
